title: Good: Clear ownership semantics documented at interface boundaries
description:
Explicit ownership documentation at critical handoff points:

1. lichen_l2.h:15-19 - Header comment documents packet ownership for both TX and RX paths
2. lichen_l2.c:262-265 - lichen_l2_send() comment states ownership contract
3. lichen_l2.c:347-352 - net_pkt_unref() call explains why L2 must free on success
4. lichen_l2.c:771-778 - net_recv_data() call documents that success transfers ownership, failure requires unref

5. lora_l2.h:154-156 - lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() warning that returned pointer aliases internal state

What goes right: The ownership model is documented where it matters - at the interface boundary. Comments explain the 'why' (Zephyr contract) not just the 'what'. The warning on get_eui64() prevents use-after-free if caller stores the pointer.

Worth defending: These comments should not be removed. Ownership bugs in network stacks cause memory leaks (if unref forgotten) or use-after-free (if double unref). The explicit documentation prevents these.
close_reason: Positive finding - pattern to preserve, not an issue
